query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-SCI Lesson 1: Scientific Method (540001)
(540001, 'The first step in the scientific method is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Form a hypothesis","Make observations and ask questions","Conduct experiments","Draw conclusions"]', 'Make observations and ask questions', 1),
(540001, 'A hypothesis is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A proven fact","A testable prediction","A final conclusion","Random guess"]', 'A testable prediction', 2),
(540001, 'The control group in an experiment:', 'multiple_choice', '["Receives the treatment","Does not receive the treatment","Is eliminated","Changes constantly"]', 'Does not receive the treatment', 3),
(540001, 'Variables that are kept constant are called:', 'multiple_choice', '["Independent variables","Dependent variables","Controlled variables","Random variables"]', 'Controlled variables', 4),
(540001, 'The independent variable is:', 'multiple_choice', '["What is measured","What is changed by the experimenter","What stays the same","The control"]', 'What is changed by the experimenter', 5),
(540001, 'The dependent variable is:', 'multiple_choice', '["What is changed","What is measured as a result","What stays constant","The hypothesis"]', 'What is measured as a result', 6),
(540001, 'A scientific theory is:', 'multiple_choice', '["An untested idea","A well-supported explanation","A random guess","A single experiment"]', 'A well-supported explanation', 7),
(540001, 'Peer review ensures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Secrecy","Quality and accuracy of research","Faster publication","Less work"]', 'Quality and accuracy of research', 8),
(540001, 'Quantitative data involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Descriptions","Numbers and measurements","Opinions","Colors"]', 'Numbers and measurements', 9),
(540001, 'Qualitative data involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Numbers only","Descriptions and observations","Calculations","Statistics"]', 'Descriptions and observations', 10),
(540001, 'Explain the steps of the scientific method and why each is important.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Steps include observation, question, hypothesis, experiment, analysis, and conclusion. Each step builds on the previous to ensure valid results.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 2: Life Science Basics (540002)
(540002, 'The basic unit of life is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Atom","Molecule","Cell","Organ"]', 'Cell', 1),
(540002, 'DNA stands for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Deoxyribonucleic acid","Dinitrogen acid","Dynamic nuclear acid","Dual nucleic acid"]', 'Deoxyribonucleic acid', 2),
(540002, 'Photosynthesis occurs in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mitochondria","Chloroplasts","Nucleus","Ribosomes"]', 'Chloroplasts', 3),
(540002, 'Cellular respiration produces:', 'multiple_choice', '["Glucose","ATP energy","Oxygen","Chlorophyll"]', 'ATP energy', 4),
(540002, 'Mitosis results in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Four different cells","Two identical cells","One larger cell","Cell death"]', 'Two identical cells', 5),
(540002, 'Genes are located on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cell membrane","Chromosomes","Ribosomes","Cytoplasm"]', 'Chromosomes', 6),
(540002, 'Natural selection favors organisms that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Are largest","Are best adapted to their environment","Reproduce least","Move fastest"]', 'Are best adapted to their environment', 7),
(540002, 'Producers in a food chain:', 'multiple_choice', '["Eat other organisms","Make their own food","Decompose matter","Hunt prey"]', 'Make their own food', 8),
(540002, 'An ecosystem includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only living things","Only non-living things","Both living and non-living things","Only plants"]', 'Both living and non-living things', 9),
(540002, 'Homeostasis is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cell division","Maintaining internal balance","Reproduction","Growth"]', 'Maintaining internal balance', 10),
(540002, 'Describe the relationship between DNA, genes, and chromosomes.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'DNA is the molecule containing genetic information, genes are segments of DNA coding for traits, and chromosomes are structures containing many genes.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 3: Physical Science Fundamentals (540003)
(540003, 'Matter is anything that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Has mass and takes up space","Is visible","Moves","Is solid"]', 'Has mass and takes up space', 1),
(540003, 'An atom consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only protons","Protons, neutrons, and electrons","Only electrons","Only neutrons"]', 'Protons, neutrons, and electrons', 2),
(540003, 'The three states of matter are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Hot, cold, warm","Solid, liquid, gas","Light, dark, medium","Fast, slow, still"]', 'Solid, liquid, gas', 3),
(540003, 'A chemical change:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is reversible","Creates new substances","Only changes shape","Requires no energy"]', 'Creates new substances', 4),
(540003, 'Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only:', 'multiple_choice', '["Eliminated","Transformed","Stopped","Duplicated"]', 'Transformed', 5),
(540003, 'Kinetic energy is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stored energy","Energy of motion","Chemical energy","Nuclear energy"]', 'Energy of motion', 6),
(540003, 'Potential energy is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Energy of motion","Stored energy","Heat energy","Light energy"]', 'Stored energy', 7),
(540003, 'Heat transfers from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cold to hot","Hot to cold","Equally in all directions","Not at all"]', 'Hot to cold', 8),
(540003, 'The periodic table organizes elements by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Color","Atomic number","Size","Age"]', 'Atomic number', 9),
(540003, 'A compound is made of:', 'multiple_choice', '["One type of atom","Two or more different elements chemically combined","Mixtures","Pure substances only"]', 'Two or more different elements chemically combined', 10),
(540003, 'Explain the difference between physical and chemical changes with examples.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Physical changes alter form but not composition (ice melting). Chemical changes create new substances (burning wood).', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 4: Earth and Space Science (540004)
(540004, 'The Earth''s layers from outside to inside are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Core, mantle, crust","Crust, mantle, core","Mantle, crust, core","Core, crust, mantle"]', 'Crust, mantle, core', 1),
(540004, 'Plate tectonics explains:', 'multiple_choice', '["Weather patterns","Movement of Earth''s crust","Ocean currents","Seasons"]', 'Movement of Earth''s crust', 2),
(540004, 'The water cycle includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only evaporation","Evaporation, condensation, precipitation","Only rain","Only clouds"]', 'Evaporation, condensation, precipitation', 3),
(540004, 'Fossils provide evidence of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Future life","Past life and evolution","Current weather","Plate movement only"]', 'Past life and evolution', 4),
(540004, 'The sun is classified as a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Planet","Star","Moon","Asteroid"]', 'Star', 5),
(540004, 'Earth''s seasons are caused by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distance from sun","Earth''s tilted axis","Moon phases","Solar flares"]', 'Earth''s tilted axis', 6),
(540004, 'The greenhouse effect:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cools the Earth","Traps heat in the atmosphere","Creates oxygen","Causes earthquakes"]', 'Traps heat in the atmosphere', 7),
(540004, 'Weathering breaks down rocks through:', 'multiple_choice', '["Physical and chemical processes","Only water","Only wind","Only heat"]', 'Physical and chemical processes', 8),
(540004, 'The rock cycle shows how rocks:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stay the same","Transform from one type to another","Only form once","Disappear"]', 'Transform from one type to another', 9),
(540004, 'Climate differs from weather because climate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Changes daily","Is long-term average conditions","Is unpredictable","Only affects temperature"]', 'Is long-term average conditions', 10),
(540004, 'Describe the water cycle and its importance to life on Earth.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The water cycle circulates water through evaporation, condensation, and precipitation, providing fresh water essential for all life.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 5: Human Body Systems (540005)
(540005, 'The circulatory system''s main function is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Digestion","Transporting blood, nutrients, and oxygen","Breathing","Movement"]', 'Transporting blood, nutrients, and oxygen', 1),
(540005, 'The respiratory system is responsible for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pumping blood","Gas exchange (oxygen and carbon dioxide)","Digesting food","Filtering waste"]', 'Gas exchange (oxygen and carbon dioxide)', 2),
(540005, 'The digestive system breaks down food into:', 'multiple_choice', '["Large pieces","Nutrients the body can absorb","Waste only","Energy directly"]', 'Nutrients the body can absorb', 3),
(540005, 'The nervous system controls:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only movement","Body functions through electrical signals","Only breathing","Only digestion"]', 'Body functions through electrical signals', 4),
(540005, 'The skeletal system provides:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only protection","Support, protection, and movement","Only movement","Only blood production"]', 'Support, protection, and movement', 5),
(540005, 'The muscular system enables:', 'multiple_choice', '["Thinking","Movement and body functions","Breathing only","Digestion only"]', 'Movement and body functions', 6),
(540005, 'The immune system:', 'multiple_choice', '["Digests food","Defends against pathogens","Pumps blood","Controls hormones"]', 'Defends against pathogens', 7),
(540005, 'The endocrine system uses:', 'multiple_choice', '["Electrical signals","Hormones to regulate body functions","Muscles","Bones"]', 'Hormones to regulate body functions', 8),
(540005, 'The excretory system removes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nutrients","Waste products from the body","Oxygen","Blood"]', 'Waste products from the body', 9),
(540005, 'Body systems work together to maintain:', 'multiple_choice', '["Constant change","Homeostasis","Imbalance","Stress"]', 'Homeostasis', 10),
(540005, 'Explain how the circulatory and respiratory systems work together.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The respiratory system brings oxygen into the lungs, where the circulatory system picks it up and delivers it to cells while removing carbon dioxide.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 6: Genetics and Heredity (540006)
(540006, 'Heredity is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Learning new skills","Passing traits from parents to offspring","Environmental adaptation","Random mutation"]', 'Passing traits from parents to offspring', 1),
(540006, 'A dominant trait:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is always expressed when present","Is never expressed","Skips generations","Requires two copies"]', 'Is always expressed when present', 2),
(540006, 'A recessive trait is expressed when:', 'multiple_choice', '["One copy is present","Two copies are present","No copies are present","It is dominant"]', 'Two copies are present', 3),
(540006, 'A Punnett square is used to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Measure DNA","Predict offspring traits","Clone organisms","Create mutations"]', 'Predict offspring traits', 4),
(540006, 'Genotype refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Physical appearance","Genetic makeup","Environment","Behavior"]', 'Genetic makeup', 5),
(540006, 'Phenotype refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Genetic code","Observable physical traits","DNA sequence","Chromosomes"]', 'Observable physical traits', 6),
(540006, 'Mutations are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always harmful","Changes in DNA sequence","Always beneficial","Impossible"]', 'Changes in DNA sequence', 7),
(540006, 'Genetic variation is important for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Uniformity","Species survival and adaptation","Cloning","Reducing diversity"]', 'Species survival and adaptation', 8),
(540006, 'Sex chromosomes determine:', 'multiple_choice', '["Height","Biological sex","Eye color","Intelligence"]', 'Biological sex', 9),
(540006, 'Selective breeding is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random mating","Choosing organisms with desired traits to reproduce","Natural selection","Mutation"]', 'Choosing organisms with desired traits to reproduce', 10),
(540006, 'Explain the difference between genotype and phenotype with an example.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Genotype is the genetic code (e.g., Bb for brown eyes), while phenotype is the observable trait (brown eye color).', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 7: Ecology and Environment (540007)
(540007, 'An ecosystem consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only animals","Living organisms and their physical environment","Only plants","Only water"]', 'Living organisms and their physical environment', 1),
(540007, 'A food web shows:', 'multiple_choice', '["One food chain","Multiple interconnected food chains","Only producers","Only consumers"]', 'Multiple interconnected food chains', 2),
(540007, 'Decomposers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Produce food","Break down dead organisms","Hunt prey","Photosynthesize"]', 'Break down dead organisms', 3),
(540007, 'The carbon cycle involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only plants","Movement of carbon through ecosystems","Only animals","Only the atmosphere"]', 'Movement of carbon through ecosystems', 4),
(540007, 'Biodiversity refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["One species","Variety of life in an ecosystem","Only plants","Only animals"]', 'Variety of life in an ecosystem', 5),
(540007, 'An invasive species:', 'multiple_choice', '["Belongs in the ecosystem","Is non-native and causes harm","Helps the ecosystem","Is always beneficial"]', 'Is non-native and causes harm', 6),
(540007, 'Symbiosis is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Competition","Close relationship between different species","Predation","Isolation"]', 'Close relationship between different species', 7),
(540007, 'Pollution affects ecosystems by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Improving them","Disrupting natural processes","Having no effect","Only affecting water"]', 'Disrupting natural processes', 8),
(540007, 'Conservation efforts aim to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Exploit resources","Protect and preserve ecosystems","Increase pollution","Reduce biodiversity"]', 'Protect and preserve ecosystems', 9),
(540007, 'Renewable resources:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cannot be replaced","Can be replenished naturally","Are unlimited","Are only fossil fuels"]', 'Can be replenished naturally', 10),
(540007, 'Describe the importance of biodiversity in ecosystems.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Biodiversity ensures ecosystem stability, provides resources, supports food webs, and enables adaptation to environmental changes.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 8: Forces and Motion (540008)
(540008, 'Newton''s First Law states that objects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always move","Stay at rest or in motion unless acted upon by a force","Always stop","Move randomly"]', 'Stay at rest or in motion unless acted upon by a force', 1),
(540008, 'Newton''s Second Law relates force to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Color","Mass and acceleration (F=ma)","Temperature","Size"]', 'Mass and acceleration (F=ma)', 2),
(540008, 'Newton''s Third Law states that for every action:', 'multiple_choice', '["There is no reaction","There is an equal and opposite reaction","There is a random reaction","Nothing happens"]', 'There is an equal and opposite reaction', 3),
(540008, 'Friction is a force that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Speeds up objects","Opposes motion","Has no effect","Only affects liquids"]', 'Opposes motion', 4),
(540008, 'Gravity is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A pushing force","An attractive force between masses","Only on Earth","Repulsive"]', 'An attractive force between masses', 5),
(540008, 'Speed is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distance × Time","Distance ÷ Time","Mass × Acceleration","Force ÷ Mass"]', 'Distance ÷ Time', 6),
(540008, 'Velocity includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Speed only","Speed and direction","Mass only","Force only"]', 'Speed and direction', 7),
(540008, 'Acceleration is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Constant speed","Change in velocity over time","Distance traveled","Mass"]', 'Change in velocity over time', 8),
(540008, 'Momentum equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mass + Velocity","Mass × Velocity","Force ÷ Time","Distance × Time"]', 'Mass × Velocity', 9),
(540008, 'Work is done when:', 'multiple_choice', '["Force is applied but nothing moves","Force moves an object","An object is at rest","No force is applied"]', 'Force moves an object', 10),
(540008, 'Explain Newton''s three laws of motion with everyday examples.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'First Law: seatbelts stop you when car stops. Second Law: pushing a cart harder makes it accelerate more. Third Law: jumping pushes Earth down as you go up.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 9: Waves and Energy (540009)
(540009, 'A wave transfers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Matter","Energy","Mass","Weight"]', 'Energy', 1),
(540009, 'The wavelength is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wave height","Distance between wave peaks","Wave speed","Wave frequency"]', 'Distance between wave peaks', 2),
(540009, 'Frequency is measured in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Meters","Hertz","Seconds","Joules"]', 'Hertz', 3),
(540009, 'Amplitude relates to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wave speed","Wave energy/intensity","Wavelength","Frequency"]', 'Wave energy/intensity', 4),
(540009, 'Sound waves are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Transverse waves","Longitudinal waves","Electromagnetic waves","Light waves"]', 'Longitudinal waves', 5),
(540009, 'Light waves are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Longitudinal waves","Electromagnetic waves","Sound waves","Mechanical waves"]', 'Electromagnetic waves', 6),
(540009, 'The electromagnetic spectrum includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only visible light","Radio waves to gamma rays","Only X-rays","Only microwaves"]', 'Radio waves to gamma rays', 7),
(540009, 'Reflection occurs when waves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pass through a material","Bounce off a surface","Bend around obstacles","Stop completely"]', 'Bounce off a surface', 8),
(540009, 'Refraction is the bending of waves when they:', 'multiple_choice', '["Hit a barrier","Change speed entering a new medium","Reflect","Stop"]', 'Change speed entering a new medium', 9),
(540009, 'The Doppler effect explains why:', 'multiple_choice', '["Light is colorful","Sound pitch changes as source moves","Waves stop","Energy is lost"]', 'Sound pitch changes as source moves', 10),
(540009, 'Describe the properties of waves and how they transfer energy.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Waves have wavelength, frequency, and amplitude. They transfer energy through oscillations without moving matter permanently.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 10: Science Test Strategies (540010)
(540010, 'When reading science passages, focus on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Every detail","Main ideas and data","Only the first sentence","Only the last sentence"]', 'Main ideas and data', 1),
(540010, 'Data tables should be read by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring headers","Identifying variables and relationships","Skipping them","Guessing"]', 'Identifying variables and relationships', 2),
(540010, 'Graphs show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random information","Relationships between variables","Only numbers","Only words"]', 'Relationships between variables', 3),
(540010, 'Scientific reasoning involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing","Drawing conclusions from evidence","Memorizing facts","Ignoring data"]', 'Drawing conclusions from evidence', 4),
(540010, 'Process of elimination helps by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Adding confusion","Narrowing down answer choices","Wasting time","Ignoring options"]', 'Narrowing down answer choices', 5),
(540010, 'Key science vocabulary should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Understood in context","Memorized without meaning","Skipped"]', 'Understood in context', 6),
(540010, 'Experimental design questions ask about:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random facts","Variables, controls, and procedures","Only results","Only conclusions"]', 'Variables, controls, and procedures', 7),
(540010, 'When unsure of an answer:', 'multiple_choice', '["Leave it blank","Make an educated guess","Give up","Skip the test"]', 'Make an educated guess', 8),
(540010, 'Time management means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on one question","Pacing through all questions","Rushing","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing through all questions', 9),
(540010, 'Reviewing answers helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Catch and correct errors","Nothing","Increase anxiety"]', 'Catch and correct errors', 10),
(540010, 'Describe strategies for interpreting scientific data and graphs on the GED.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Read titles and labels, identify variables, look for trends and patterns, and connect data to the question being asked.', 11);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-SCI Lesson 1: Scientific Method (540001)
(540001, 'The first step in the scientific method is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Form a hypothesis","Make observations and ask questions","Conduct experiments","Draw conclusions"]', 'Make observations and ask questions', 1),
(540001, 'A hypothesis is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A proven fact","A testable prediction","A final conclusion","Random guess"]', 'A testable prediction', 2),
(540001, 'The control group in an experiment:', 'multiple_choice', '["Receives the treatment","Does not receive the treatment","Is eliminated","Changes constantly"]', 'Does not receive the treatment', 3),
(540001, 'Variables that are kept constant are called:', 'multiple_choice', '["Independent variables","Dependent variables","Controlled variables","Random variables"]', 'Controlled variables', 4),
(540001, 'The independent variable is:', 'multiple_choice', '["What is measured","What is changed by the experimenter","What stays the same","The control"]', 'What is changed by the experimenter', 5),
(540001, 'The dependent variable is:', 'multiple_choice', '["What is changed","What is measured as a result","What stays constant","The hypothesis"]', 'What is measured as a result', 6),
(540001, 'A scientific theory is:', 'multiple_choice', '["An untested idea","A well-supported explanation","A random guess","A single experiment"]', 'A well-supported explanation', 7),
(540001, 'Peer review ensures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Secrecy","Quality and accuracy of research","Faster publication","Less work"]', 'Quality and accuracy of research', 8),
(540001, 'Quantitative data involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Descriptions","Numbers and measurements","Opinions","Colors"]', 'Numbers and measurements', 9),
(540001, 'Qualitative data involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Numbers only","Descriptions and observations","Calculations","Statistics"]', 'Descriptions and observations', 10),
(540001, 'Explain the steps of the scientific method and why each is important.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Steps include observation, question, hypothesis, experiment, analysis, and conclusion. Each step builds on the previous to ensure valid results.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 2: Life Science Basics (540002)
(540002, 'The basic unit of life is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Atom","Molecule","Cell","Organ"]', 'Cell', 1),
(540002, 'DNA stands for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Deoxyribonucleic acid","Dinitrogen acid","Dynamic nuclear acid","Dual nucleic acid"]', 'Deoxyribonucleic acid', 2),
(540002, 'Photosynthesis occurs in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mitochondria","Chloroplasts","Nucleus","Ribosomes"]', 'Chloroplasts', 3),
(540002, 'Cellular respiration produces:', 'multiple_choice', '["Glucose","ATP energy","Oxygen","Chlorophyll"]', 'ATP energy', 4),
(540002, 'Mitosis results in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Four different cells","Two identical cells","One larger cell","Cell death"]', 'Two identical cells', 5),
(540002, 'Genes are located on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cell membrane","Chromosomes","Ribosomes","Cytoplasm"]', 'Chromosomes', 6),
(540002, 'Natural selection favors organisms that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Are largest","Are best adapted to their environment","Reproduce least","Move fastest"]', 'Are best adapted to their environment', 7),
(540002, 'Producers in a food chain:', 'multiple_choice', '["Eat other organisms","Make their own food","Decompose matter","Hunt prey"]', 'Make their own food', 8),
(540002, 'An ecosystem includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only living things","Only non-living things","Both living and non-living things","Only plants"]', 'Both living and non-living things', 9),
(540002, 'Homeostasis is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cell division","Maintaining internal balance","Reproduction","Growth"]', 'Maintaining internal balance', 10),
(540002, 'Describe the relationship between DNA, genes, and chromosomes.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'DNA is the molecule containing genetic information, genes are segments of DNA coding for traits, and chromosomes are structures containing many genes.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 3: Physical Science Fundamentals (540003)
(540003, 'Matter is anything that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Has mass and takes up space","Is visible","Moves","Is solid"]', 'Has mass and takes up space', 1),
(540003, 'An atom consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only protons","Protons, neutrons, and electrons","Only electrons","Only neutrons"]', 'Protons, neutrons, and electrons', 2),
(540003, 'The three states of matter are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Hot, cold, warm","Solid, liquid, gas","Light, dark, medium","Fast, slow, still"]', 'Solid, liquid, gas', 3),
(540003, 'A chemical change:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is reversible","Creates new substances","Only changes shape","Requires no energy"]', 'Creates new substances', 4),
(540003, 'Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only:', 'multiple_choice', '["Eliminated","Transformed","Stopped","Duplicated"]', 'Transformed', 5),
(540003, 'Kinetic energy is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stored energy","Energy of motion","Chemical energy","Nuclear energy"]', 'Energy of motion', 6),
(540003, 'Potential energy is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Energy of motion","Stored energy","Heat energy","Light energy"]', 'Stored energy', 7),
(540003, 'Heat transfers from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cold to hot","Hot to cold","Equally in all directions","Not at all"]', 'Hot to cold', 8),
(540003, 'The periodic table organizes elements by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Color","Atomic number","Size","Age"]', 'Atomic number', 9),
(540003, 'A compound is made of:', 'multiple_choice', '["One type of atom","Two or more different elements chemically combined","Mixtures","Pure substances only"]', 'Two or more different elements chemically combined', 10),
(540003, 'Explain the difference between physical and chemical changes with examples.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Physical changes alter form but not composition (ice melting). Chemical changes create new substances (burning wood).', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 4: Earth and Space Science (540004)
(540004, 'The Earth''s layers from outside to inside are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Core, mantle, crust","Crust, mantle, core","Mantle, crust, core","Core, crust, mantle"]', 'Crust, mantle, core', 1),
(540004, 'Plate tectonics explains:', 'multiple_choice', '["Weather patterns","Movement of Earth''s crust","Ocean currents","Seasons"]', 'Movement of Earth''s crust', 2),
(540004, 'The water cycle includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only evaporation","Evaporation, condensation, precipitation","Only rain","Only clouds"]', 'Evaporation, condensation, precipitation', 3),
(540004, 'Fossils provide evidence of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Future life","Past life and evolution","Current weather","Plate movement only"]', 'Past life and evolution', 4),
(540004, 'The sun is classified as a:', 'multiple_choice', '["Planet","Star","Moon","Asteroid"]', 'Star', 5),
(540004, 'Earth''s seasons are caused by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distance from sun","Earth''s tilted axis","Moon phases","Solar flares"]', 'Earth''s tilted axis', 6),
(540004, 'The greenhouse effect:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cools the Earth","Traps heat in the atmosphere","Creates oxygen","Causes earthquakes"]', 'Traps heat in the atmosphere', 7),
(540004, 'Weathering breaks down rocks through:', 'multiple_choice', '["Physical and chemical processes","Only water","Only wind","Only heat"]', 'Physical and chemical processes', 8),
(540004, 'The rock cycle shows how rocks:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stay the same","Transform from one type to another","Only form once","Disappear"]', 'Transform from one type to another', 9),
(540004, 'Climate differs from weather because climate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Changes daily","Is long-term average conditions","Is unpredictable","Only affects temperature"]', 'Is long-term average conditions', 10),
(540004, 'Describe the water cycle and its importance to life on Earth.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The water cycle circulates water through evaporation, condensation, and precipitation, providing fresh water essential for all life.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 5: Human Body Systems (540005)
(540005, 'The circulatory system''s main function is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Digestion","Transporting blood, nutrients, and oxygen","Breathing","Movement"]', 'Transporting blood, nutrients, and oxygen', 1),
(540005, 'The respiratory system is responsible for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pumping blood","Gas exchange (oxygen and carbon dioxide)","Digesting food","Filtering waste"]', 'Gas exchange (oxygen and carbon dioxide)', 2),
(540005, 'The digestive system breaks down food into:', 'multiple_choice', '["Large pieces","Nutrients the body can absorb","Waste only","Energy directly"]', 'Nutrients the body can absorb', 3),
(540005, 'The nervous system controls:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only movement","Body functions through electrical signals","Only breathing","Only digestion"]', 'Body functions through electrical signals', 4),
(540005, 'The skeletal system provides:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only protection","Support, protection, and movement","Only movement","Only blood production"]', 'Support, protection, and movement', 5),
(540005, 'The muscular system enables:', 'multiple_choice', '["Thinking","Movement and body functions","Breathing only","Digestion only"]', 'Movement and body functions', 6),
(540005, 'The immune system:', 'multiple_choice', '["Digests food","Defends against pathogens","Pumps blood","Controls hormones"]', 'Defends against pathogens', 7),
(540005, 'The endocrine system uses:', 'multiple_choice', '["Electrical signals","Hormones to regulate body functions","Muscles","Bones"]', 'Hormones to regulate body functions', 8),
(540005, 'The excretory system removes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nutrients","Waste products from the body","Oxygen","Blood"]', 'Waste products from the body', 9),
(540005, 'Body systems work together to maintain:', 'multiple_choice', '["Constant change","Homeostasis","Imbalance","Stress"]', 'Homeostasis', 10),
(540005, 'Explain how the circulatory and respiratory systems work together.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The respiratory system brings oxygen into the lungs, where the circulatory system picks it up and delivers it to cells while removing carbon dioxide.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 6: Genetics and Heredity (540006)
(540006, 'Heredity is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Learning new skills","Passing traits from parents to offspring","Environmental adaptation","Random mutation"]', 'Passing traits from parents to offspring', 1),
(540006, 'A dominant trait:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is always expressed when present","Is never expressed","Skips generations","Requires two copies"]', 'Is always expressed when present', 2),
(540006, 'A recessive trait is expressed when:', 'multiple_choice', '["One copy is present","Two copies are present","No copies are present","It is dominant"]', 'Two copies are present', 3),
(540006, 'A Punnett square is used to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Measure DNA","Predict offspring traits","Clone organisms","Create mutations"]', 'Predict offspring traits', 4),
(540006, 'Genotype refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Physical appearance","Genetic makeup","Environment","Behavior"]', 'Genetic makeup', 5),
(540006, 'Phenotype refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Genetic code","Observable physical traits","DNA sequence","Chromosomes"]', 'Observable physical traits', 6),
(540006, 'Mutations are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always harmful","Changes in DNA sequence","Always beneficial","Impossible"]', 'Changes in DNA sequence', 7),
(540006, 'Genetic variation is important for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Uniformity","Species survival and adaptation","Cloning","Reducing diversity"]', 'Species survival and adaptation', 8),
(540006, 'Sex chromosomes determine:', 'multiple_choice', '["Height","Biological sex","Eye color","Intelligence"]', 'Biological sex', 9),
(540006, 'Selective breeding is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random mating","Choosing organisms with desired traits to reproduce","Natural selection","Mutation"]', 'Choosing organisms with desired traits to reproduce', 10),
(540006, 'Explain the difference between genotype and phenotype with an example.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Genotype is the genetic code (e.g., Bb for brown eyes), while phenotype is the observable trait (brown eye color).', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 7: Ecology and Environment (540007)
(540007, 'An ecosystem consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only animals","Living organisms and their physical environment","Only plants","Only water"]', 'Living organisms and their physical environment', 1),
(540007, 'A food web shows:', 'multiple_choice', '["One food chain","Multiple interconnected food chains","Only producers","Only consumers"]', 'Multiple interconnected food chains', 2),
(540007, 'Decomposers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Produce food","Break down dead organisms","Hunt prey","Photosynthesize"]', 'Break down dead organisms', 3),
(540007, 'The carbon cycle involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only plants","Movement of carbon through ecosystems","Only animals","Only the atmosphere"]', 'Movement of carbon through ecosystems', 4),
(540007, 'Biodiversity refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["One species","Variety of life in an ecosystem","Only plants","Only animals"]', 'Variety of life in an ecosystem', 5),
(540007, 'An invasive species:', 'multiple_choice', '["Belongs in the ecosystem","Is non-native and causes harm","Helps the ecosystem","Is always beneficial"]', 'Is non-native and causes harm', 6),
(540007, 'Symbiosis is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Competition","Close relationship between different species","Predation","Isolation"]', 'Close relationship between different species', 7),
(540007, 'Pollution affects ecosystems by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Improving them","Disrupting natural processes","Having no effect","Only affecting water"]', 'Disrupting natural processes', 8),
(540007, 'Conservation efforts aim to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Exploit resources","Protect and preserve ecosystems","Increase pollution","Reduce biodiversity"]', 'Protect and preserve ecosystems', 9),
(540007, 'Renewable resources:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cannot be replaced","Can be replenished naturally","Are unlimited","Are only fossil fuels"]', 'Can be replenished naturally', 10),
(540007, 'Describe the importance of biodiversity in ecosystems.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Biodiversity ensures ecosystem stability, provides resources, supports food webs, and enables adaptation to environmental changes.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 8: Forces and Motion (540008)
(540008, 'Newton''s First Law states that objects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always move","Stay at rest or in motion unless acted upon by a force","Always stop","Move randomly"]', 'Stay at rest or in motion unless acted upon by a force', 1),
(540008, 'Newton''s Second Law relates force to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Color","Mass and acceleration (F=ma)","Temperature","Size"]', 'Mass and acceleration (F=ma)', 2),
(540008, 'Newton''s Third Law states that for every action:', 'multiple_choice', '["There is no reaction","There is an equal and opposite reaction","There is a random reaction","Nothing happens"]', 'There is an equal and opposite reaction', 3),
(540008, 'Friction is a force that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Speeds up objects","Opposes motion","Has no effect","Only affects liquids"]', 'Opposes motion', 4),
(540008, 'Gravity is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A pushing force","An attractive force between masses","Only on Earth","Repulsive"]', 'An attractive force between masses', 5),
(540008, 'Speed is calculated by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distance × Time","Distance ÷ Time","Mass × Acceleration","Force ÷ Mass"]', 'Distance ÷ Time', 6),
(540008, 'Velocity includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Speed only","Speed and direction","Mass only","Force only"]', 'Speed and direction', 7),
(540008, 'Acceleration is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Constant speed","Change in velocity over time","Distance traveled","Mass"]', 'Change in velocity over time', 8),
(540008, 'Momentum equals:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mass + Velocity","Mass × Velocity","Force ÷ Time","Distance × Time"]', 'Mass × Velocity', 9),
(540008, 'Work is done when:', 'multiple_choice', '["Force is applied but nothing moves","Force moves an object","An object is at rest","No force is applied"]', 'Force moves an object', 10),
(540008, 'Explain Newton''s three laws of motion with everyday examples.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'First Law: seatbelts stop you when car stops. Second Law: pushing a cart harder makes it accelerate more. Third Law: jumping pushes Earth down as you go up.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 9: Waves and Energy (540009)
(540009, 'A wave transfers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Matter","Energy","Mass","Weight"]', 'Energy', 1),
(540009, 'The wavelength is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wave height","Distance between wave peaks","Wave speed","Wave frequency"]', 'Distance between wave peaks', 2),
(540009, 'Frequency is measured in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Meters","Hertz","Seconds","Joules"]', 'Hertz', 3),
(540009, 'Amplitude relates to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wave speed","Wave energy/intensity","Wavelength","Frequency"]', 'Wave energy/intensity', 4),
(540009, 'Sound waves are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Transverse waves","Longitudinal waves","Electromagnetic waves","Light waves"]', 'Longitudinal waves', 5),
(540009, 'Light waves are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Longitudinal waves","Electromagnetic waves","Sound waves","Mechanical waves"]', 'Electromagnetic waves', 6),
(540009, 'The electromagnetic spectrum includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only visible light","Radio waves to gamma rays","Only X-rays","Only microwaves"]', 'Radio waves to gamma rays', 7),
(540009, 'Reflection occurs when waves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pass through a material","Bounce off a surface","Bend around obstacles","Stop completely"]', 'Bounce off a surface', 8),
(540009, 'Refraction is the bending of waves when they:', 'multiple_choice', '["Hit a barrier","Change speed entering a new medium","Reflect","Stop"]', 'Change speed entering a new medium', 9),
(540009, 'The Doppler effect explains why:', 'multiple_choice', '["Light is colorful","Sound pitch changes as source moves","Waves stop","Energy is lost"]', 'Sound pitch changes as source moves', 10),
(540009, 'Describe the properties of waves and how they transfer energy.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Waves have wavelength, frequency, and amplitude. They transfer energy through oscillations without moving matter permanently.', 11),

-- GED-SCI Lesson 10: Science Test Strategies (540010)
(540010, 'When reading science passages, focus on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Every detail","Main ideas and data","Only the first sentence","Only the last sentence"]', 'Main ideas and data', 1),
(540010, 'Data tables should be read by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring headers","Identifying variables and relationships","Skipping them","Guessing"]', 'Identifying variables and relationships', 2),
(540010, 'Graphs show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random information","Relationships between variables","Only numbers","Only words"]', 'Relationships between variables', 3),
(540010, 'Scientific reasoning involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing","Drawing conclusions from evidence","Memorizing facts","Ignoring data"]', 'Drawing conclusions from evidence', 4),
(540010, 'Process of elimination helps by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Adding confusion","Narrowing down answer choices","Wasting time","Ignoring options"]', 'Narrowing down answer choices', 5),
(540010, 'Key science vocabulary should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Understood in context","Memorized without meaning","Skipped"]', 'Understood in context', 6),
(540010, 'Experimental design questions ask about:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random facts","Variables, controls, and procedures","Only results","Only conclusions"]', 'Variables, controls, and procedures', 7),
(540010, 'When unsure of an answer:', 'multiple_choice', '["Leave it blank","Make an educated guess","Give up","Skip the test"]', 'Make an educated guess', 8),
(540010, 'Time management means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on one question","Pacing through all questions","Rushing","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing through all questions', 9),
(540010, 'Reviewing answers helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Catch and correct errors","Nothing","Increase anxiety"]', 'Catch and correct errors', 10),
(540010, 'Describe strategies for interpreting scientific data and graphs on the GED.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Read titles and labels, identify variables, look for trends and patterns, and connect data to the question being asked.', 11);
